sourceMovie.title: Mortal Engines
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Mortal Engines (2018) stuck because it commits to its adventure premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Christian Rivers's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The adventure and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Mortal Engines clicked for you, Mad Max: Fury Road (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Mortal Engines?
faq.answer: Try Mad Max: Fury Road, The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2, Valerian and the City of a Thousand Planets—each shares DNA with Mortal Engines while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Mortal Engines?
faq.answer: Mortal Engines (2018) is best described as Adventure, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Mortal Engines worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
